fix_guidance: Use >= instead of == for MAX_INV_SZ truncation check. If size calculation changes, == could miss oversized responses.
note: Three call sites (L6046, L6098, L6155) check vInv.size() == MAX_INV_SZ. If future code changes cause exact equality to be missed, oversized INV could slip through.

rule_name: RdRand Silent Fallback to Zero Entropy
fix_guidance: Consider adding a hard failure or logging when all 10 RdRand attempts fail. Currently returns 0 silently.
known_exploits: Blockchain.info Android weak RNG (2015) - different codebase but same class of issue
note: RdRand retries 10 times but if ALL fail, returns r1=0 (initialized to 0 for compiler warning suppression). This is extremely unlikely but theoretically could produce deterministic output.

rule_name: assert() in Consensus Path (No Graceful Degradation)
fix_guidance: Production consensus code uses assert() which is compiled out in release builds. Consider replacing with non-abort checks.
note: Bitcoin Core uses assert() extensively in consensus code (L207, L250 in validation.cpp). While these are internal invariants, a bug could cause silent corruption in release mode if asserts are disabled.

fix_guidance: Consider BIP-69 or random input ordering to reduce transaction graph analysis.
note: Coin selection (coinselection.cpp) uses deterministic algorithms like BnB and Knapsack. Input/output ordering can leak wallet behavior to chain analysis. BIP-69 (lexicographic ordering) not implemented.

rule_name: Fee Sniping via Predictable Coin Selection
fix_guidance: mitigate fee sniping by occasionally picking lower-fee rate inputs and shifting change outputs.
note: Bitcoin Core does NOT implement anti fee-sniping measures in its default coin selection. Transaction fees can be predicted and front-run by miners.

rule_name: Limited Fuzz Coverage for P2P Message Handlers
fix_guidance: Expand fuzz testing coverage to all P2P message handlers. Currently only a subset are fuzzed.
note: While Bitcoin Core has fuzz tests (src/test/fuzz/), not all P2P message types in net_processing.cpp have dedicated fuzz harnesses. Missing fuzz coverage is the #1 source of Bitcoin CVEs historically.

fix_guidance: BIP-324 (v2 P2P transport) with EllSwift key exchange is relatively new. Ensure it has received sufficient cryptographic review and fuzzing.
note: EllSwift was added in 2023 for BIP-324 encrypted P2P transport. New cryptographic code should have extra scrutiny. The implementation lives in the secp256k1 submodule.
